pr.title: Add docs/README.md: process overview and documentation index
pr.body:
Issue #2 requests a README in `docs/` to serve as an onboarding entry point to OctoAcme's project management knowledge base.

## Changes

- **`docs/README.md`** (new file)
  - 4-paragraph prose overview covering the full project lifecycle, roles, execution rhythm, and QA practices — adapted from the conversation summary
  - Markdown table indexing all eight process docs with a one-line description each, linking directly to each file in the same folder

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

Create a pull request in repository `SetarehStarMS/skills-scale-institutional-knowledge-using-copilot-spaces2` to address issue #2: https://github.com/SetarehStarMS/skills-scale-institutional-knowledge-using-copilot-spaces2/issues/2

Issue title: `[Process Doc Update]: Add README with process summary and links to all docs in the docs folder`

Requested change:
- Add a new README file in the `docs/` folder.
- The README should serve as a clear entry point to the project management process documentation.
- Include a brief overview of the project management processes used by OctoAcme, based on the summary created in this conversation.
- Then include a list of links to all process documentation files in `docs/`.

Use this summary as the basis for the overview section, adapting it into README-friendly prose:

"OctoAcme uses a lightweight but structured project management approach that guides work from initiation through planning, execution, release, and retrospective improvement. Projects begin with a clear business need, success metrics, stakeholder alignment, an initial risk list, and a lightweight one-pager to support go/no-go decisions. Once approved, planning focuses on turning the initiative into an actionable backlog with acceptance criteria, estimates, dependencies, milestones, a definition of done, and an initial QA approach. This creates a repeatable lifecycle in which teams move from idea validation to delivery readiness with shared expectations and documented artifacts.

The process depends on clearly defined roles working together across the project lifecycle. Project Managers coordinate delivery, schedules, risks, meetings, and stakeholder communication; Product Managers define outcomes, prioritize backlog items, and measure success; Developers implement features, contribute to estimation and planning, and help identify technical risks; QA or testing contributors validate quality and acceptance criteria; and stakeholders provide input and approvals. OctoAcme’s personas documentation reinforces these responsibilities and positions each role as part of a collaborative, cross-functional delivery model with clear ownership and accountability.

Execution is managed through a regular operating rhythm and visible workflow practices. Teams use project boards with stages such as Backlog, Ready, In Progress, In Review, QA, and Done, supported by daily or frequent standups, weekly delivery or PM/Product syncs, sprint-end demos, and monthly stakeholder updates. Risks and dependencies are tracked through a risk register and discussed in weekly syncs, while escalation paths move from team-level triage to PM and Product Lead involvement and then to sponsor-level escalation for business-critical issues. Communication emphasizes a single source of truth, regular status reporting, milestone-based updates, and incident-specific communication when urgent problems arise.

Quality assurance is built into both development and release practices rather than treated as a final checkpoint. During execution, teams are expected to use small pull requests when possible, include issue links and acceptance criteria in PR descriptions, run automated tests and linting in CI, and obtain at least one approval before merging. QA expectations include unit tests for new logic, integration tests where needed, end-to-end smoke tests for critical flows, security scanning in CI, and manual QA for feature acceptance when appropriate. Before release, OctoAcme requires completed acceptance criteria, passing CI and security checks, release notes, rollback planning, smoke test preparation, and post-deployment verification, followed by retrospectives that convert lessons learned into tracked improvement actions."

Required docs index links to include:
- `octoacme-project-management-overview.md`
- `octoacme-project-initiation.md`
- `octoacme-project-planning.md`
- `octoacme-execution-and-tracking.md`
- `octoacme-risks-and-communication.md`
- `octoacme-release-and-deployment.md`
- `octoacme-retrospective-and-continuous-improvement.md`
- `octoacme-roles-and-personas.md`

Implementation notes:
- Put the README at `docs/README.md`.
- Make the tone concise, welcoming, and useful for onboarding.
- The PR description should reference and close issue #2.
- Add `SetarehStarMS` as a reviewer on the pull request.
- Add the created pull request to the issue as a linked development item / relationship if supported by the agent workflow.

The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):

User: - Create a 3-4 paragraph summary of the project management processes used by OctoAcme
  based on the documentation in the docs folder of this repository.
- Focus on key workflows, personas/roles, communication strategies, and quality assurance practices.

Assistant: OctoAcme uses a lightweight but structured project management approach that guides work from initiation through planning, execution, release, and retrospe...

</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

*This pull request was created from Copilot chat.*
>
